title: [InstCombine] Refactor the code for folding logicop and sext/zext. NFC.
summary:
The developers generalized the pattern by recognizing that `and`/`or`/`xor` of matching extends (`zext`/`sext`) can be narrowed to operate on the smaller source types, even when those source types differ in bit width. The key insight is that if both casts are single-use, you can upcast the narrower source to match the wider one, perform the logic op at that intermediate width, and then extend to the original destination type. This eliminates redundant extension bits and reduces operation width.

The patch expands the optimization from the original specific case (where source types matched) to handle:
- Mixed extend types (`zext` with `sext`)
- Different source bit widths (e.g., `i8` and `i4`)
- Vector types
- All bitwise logic ops (`and`, `or`, `xor`)

Crucially, it relaxes the "both casts must have one use" requirement to "at least one cast has one use" for equal-width sources, but requires both to have one use when converting between different widths to avoid introducing extra casts. This balances optimization power with instruction count control.
